2606.24842.title: World Models in Pieces: Structural Certification for General Agents
2606.24842.paper_summary: In the big-world regime, agents cannot be universally capable and their ability is inevitably specialized across a world model in pieces. Consequently, standard uniform guarantees fail to distinguish between the understanding of critical bottlenecks and irrelevant failures. We first formalize this limitation by proving that general agents are not universal, rendering standard worst-case analysis uninformative. To overcome this, we introduce structural certification, a transition-local framework that maps bounded goal-conditioned performance to entry-wise guarantees on the agent's internal world model. Our main contribution is constructive. We provide algorithms that filter specific transitions using deep compositional goals and prove that a general agent on these goals has a structural world model with a $\mathcal{O}(1/n) + \mathcal{O}(δ)$ error bound. Conversely, this bound is tight in the small-$δ$ regime, whose existence is explicitly guaranteed by our certification. These results enable the certifiable deployment of general agents by localizing the specific transitions where long-horizon planning is reliable.
2606.24842.paper_summary_zh: 在大世界體系中，代理無法具備普遍能力，其能力不可避免地在世界模型中專門化為片段。因此，標準的統一保證無法區分對關鍵瓶頸的理解和無關的失敗。我們首先通過證明一般代理並非普遍的來形式化這一限制，使得標準的最壞情況分析變得無法提供信息。為了克服這一點，我們引入結構性認證，這是一個過渡局部框架，將有界的目標條件性能映射到代理內部世界模型的逐項保證。我們的主要貢獻是建設性的。我們提供了使用深度組合目標過濾特定過渡的算法，並證明在這些目標上的一般代理擁有一個結構性世界模型，其誤差界限為 $\mathcal{O}(1/n) + \mathcal{O}(δ)$。相反，這一界限在小-$δ$ 體系中是緊的，其存在性由我們的認證明確保。這些結果通過定位長期規劃可靠的特定過渡，使得一般代理的可認證部署成為可能。
2606.24842.author: Yikai Lu et.al.
2606.24842.authors: Yikai Lu, Yifei Wu, Xinyu Lu, Tongxin Li
